title: You Break It, You Own It? Not for Trump When It Comes to Iran.
summary: “We’ll see what happens with the people,” President Trump said as he appeared to be distancing himself from the longer-term consequences of the war in Iran.

title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s Son Emerges as Leading Choice to Be His Successor
summary: If Mojtaba Khamenei is chosen by Iran’s senior clerics, it may signal a victory for hard-liners, an analyst said.

summary: Who will lead Iran once the war is over? “Most of the people we had in mind are dead,” says President Trump.

summary: Midterm season is kicking off with hard-fought Senate and House primary contests that include battles over political style and ideology, age and scandal.

summary: Officers appeared to find magazines for a rifle when searching his car, but details about why he was taken into custody were not immediately available.

summary: Disturbing images released in the Epstein files showing passages from Nabokov’s infamous novel written on bodies exemplify a world where women and girls are treated as objects for consumption.

summary: In Springfield, Ohio, some Americans have converted their basements and spare bedrooms into shelters for immigrant families who could be targeted in raids.

summary: Judge Michael E. Farbiarz warned in an order on Monday that he may have to hold the Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency in criminal contempt.

summary: The ultrawealthy are vying for a limited number of exclusive properties on the islands and shorelines of South Florida.

title: Apalachee High School Shooting: Colin Gray Found Guilty of Murder

summary: The National Capital Planning Commission received about 32,000 messages during its public comment period. Suffice it to say: Many people are not happy with the president’s ballroom plans.

summary: The two aides to Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer had been on leave during an investigation of misconduct at the department.

summary: Stephen Grosz’s books show a new generation the inner workings of psychoanalysis.

summary: Friedrich Merz has called for greater European unity, but he did not publicly object to the president rebuking Spain and Britain during an Oval Office meeting.

summary: In the wake of the U.S. bombing of Iran and its dismissal of European allies, an anxious continent’s best chance at security runs through its largest economy.

summary: Attorney General Letitia James is investigating the university for its actions after the 2012 arrest of Robert Hadden, a former Columbia gynecologist convicted of sex crimes.
